scope1: Scope 1 — direct GHG emissions from sources owned or controlled by the organisation: stationary combustion (boilers, kilns), mobile combustion (cars, forklifts), process emissions (e.g. calcium carbonate decarbonation during ceramic firing), fugitive emissions (F-gas refrigerant leaks). Reference: GHG Protocol Corporate Standard §4.
scope2: Scope 2 — indirect emissions from generation of purchased electricity, heat, steam or cooling consumed by the organisation. Reported under two mandatory variants: Location-Based (LB) and Market-Based (MB). Reference: GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance.
scope2_lb: Scope 2 Location-Based — emissions from purchased electricity calculated using the average emission factor of the grid where consumption takes place (for Italy: ISPRA, latest edition of the report on CO2 emissions in the electricity sector). Reflects the physical grid mix and does not recognise contractual instruments (GO, PPA).
scope2_mb: Scope 2 Market-Based — emissions from purchased electricity calculated by recognising documented contractual instruments. Selection follows the GHG Protocol Scope 2 instrument hierarchy: (1) energy attribute certificates (GO/REC), (2) direct contracts (PPA), (3) supplier-specific rate, (4) national residual mix (AIB European Residual Mix), (5) grid average as last resort. Allows the benefit of renewable electricity purchases to be shown.
scope3: Scope 3 — indirect value-chain emissions not covered by Scope 2, split into 15 numbered categories (Cat. 1 Purchased goods, Cat. 2 Capital goods, Cat. 3 Fuel-and-energy/WTT, Cat. 4 Upstream transport, Cat. 5 Waste, Cat. 6 Business travel, Cat. 7 Employee commuting, Cat. 8 Upstream leased assets, Cat. 9 Downstream transport, Cat. 10 Processing of sold products, Cat. 11 Use of sold products, Cat. 12 End-of-life, Cat. 13 Downstream leased assets, Cat. 14 Franchises, Cat. 15 Investments). Reference: GHG Protocol Scope 3 Standard.
biogenic: Biogenic CO2 — carbon dioxide from combustion/decomposition of biomass and biogas (carbon fixed by the biosphere in the short cycle). Per ADR-007, GHG Protocol Corporate Standard and ESRS E1 it is reported as a memo disclosure alongside E1-6 and is NOT summed into Scope 1/2/3 totals. The non-CO2 components of biomass combustion (CH4, N2O) remain in Scope 1.
tco2e: Tonne of CO2 equivalent (tCO2e) — unit expressing the climate impact of a greenhouse gas in terms of carbon dioxide with equal 100-year warming power. Obtained by multiplying the mass of the gas by its GWP100 factor.
gwp: GWP — Global Warming Potential. Coefficient measuring how much a greenhouse gas contributes to global warming compared to CO2 over a time horizon (typically 100 years → GWP100). E.g. fossil CH4 = 29.8 (AR6) / 28 (AR5); non-fossil CH4 = 27.0 (AR6). CSRD ESRS E1 uses AR6.
gwp_ar6: AR6 — GWP set from the IPCC Sixth Assessment Report (2021). Reference set for CSRD ESRS E1. AR6 distinguishes fossil CH4 (GWP100 = 29.8) from non-fossil CH4 (GWP100 = 27.0); N2O = 273; SF6 = 25,200. Lacking source disaggregation for CH4, the tool applies an operational mean of 27.9 and declares it in the Methodology Statement.
gwp_ar5: AR5 — GWP set from the IPCC Fifth Assessment Report (2014). Still required by some regulatory schemes (e.g. EU ETS Phase IV for verification of pre-2024 historical emissions). E.g. CH4=28, N2O=265.
factor_source: Source of the emission factor used in the calculation. Official sources are preferred: ISPRA (IT electricity grid), IEA (foreign grids), DEFRA (UK/international fuels and transport), AIB (European residual mix), IPCC Guidelines (default factors), ecoinvent/EXIOBASE (cradle-to-gate LCA). Source traceability is required for ISAE 3000 and ISO 14064-3 audit.
factor_version: Version/publication year of the emission factor. Factors are updated annually; the exact version used in the calculation is recorded on the emission row (append-only) to enable later reproducibility of the report.
methodology: Calculation methodology applied. For CSRD it refers to the GHG Protocol Corporate Standard (2004 with revisions); for Scope 2 it distinguishes Location-Based vs Market-Based; for Scope 3 to the GHG Protocol Scope 3 Standard. For EU ETS, Regulations (EU) 2018/2066 (Monitoring) and 2018/2067 (Verification) apply.
codice_sito: Unique site/plant identifier. The 'Operational Control' organisational boundary groups multiple sites under the same corporate inventory. For CSRD ESRS E1 the minimum disclosure level is the consolidated total; per-site detail is voluntary information.
anno_fiscale: Reporting year: a 12-month consecutive period the data refer to. For CSRD it usually coincides with the financial year. The base year (2024 in this tool) is the reference for multi-year comparisons.
intensity_revenue: Carbon intensity per revenue — emissions per unit of turnover. Required by ESRS E1-6 §53. The exact unit is at the preparer's discretion provided it is declared and consistent over time; this tool adopts tCO2e per million EUR (tCO2e/MEUR). Allows climate performance to be compared net of economic growth.
intensity_m2: Intensity per production area — kgCO2e per m² of product (porcelain stoneware). Physical indicator typical of the ceramics sector, complementary to the economic one.
intensity_fte: Intensity per FTE — tCO2e per full-time-equivalent employee. Organisational indicator required by some frameworks (e.g. SBTi targets).
intensity_kg_product: Intensity per product mass — kgCO2e per tonne of finished product (porcelain stoneware). Metric used for sector benchmarking and for Environmental Product Declarations (EPD).
yoy_delta_abs: Year-over-Year absolute Δ — difference in tCO2e between the comparison year and the base year. Shows the actual volume of emissions changed.
yoy_delta_rel: Year-over-Year relative Δ — percentage change between comparison year and base year. Variations above ±20% generally require an explanatory note (capacity change, exceptional events).
dq_crit: DQ-CRIT — CRITICAL data-quality finding (blocking violation). Examples: site coverage <90%, negative values on physical quantities, outliers z>4, temporal gaps >5%. Blocks the calculation until resolved or formally waived with justification.
dq_warn: DQ-WARN — WARNING data-quality finding (non-blocking). Examples: values marginally outside historical range, data quality flagged 'Estimated' (E) instead of 'Provisional' (P), partial datasets. Must be reviewed and annotated but does not prevent calculation closure.
dq_status_open: OPEN — the finding is open and awaiting triage by the data steward or ESG manager.
dq_status_waived: WAIVED — the ESG manager has accepted the finding as known and recorded the justification (audit trail), without modifying the data. Typical for real anomalies (e.g. production stoppage).
dq_status_remediated: REMEDIATED — the finding has been resolved by correcting the raw data or re-running the calc pipeline. Corrections create new append-only rows with a pointer to the predecessor.
audit_correlation_id: Correlation ID — UUID linking all rows (raw, emissions, DQ findings) produced by the same ETL/calc pipeline run. Used to reconstruct an end-to-end calculation during ISAE 3000 audit.
audit_predecessor: Predecessor ID (superseded_by) — when an emission row is corrected, the new row points via this field to the one it replaces. Historical rows are NEVER deleted (append-only ledger): the audit can always reconstruct the value timeline.
audit_valid_from_to: Bitemporal validity — valid_from / valid_to indicate the interval in which a row is considered 'current'. A correction opens a new row with new valid_from and closes the previous one by setting its valid_to. The current value is the one with valid_to=NULL.
go_certificate: GO — Guarantee of Origin. Electronic certificate issued by GSE (in Italy) attesting the renewable origin of 1 MWh of electricity. For ESRS E1 and GHG Protocol Scope 2 MB the use of GOs must be documented; without evidence the residual mix applies.
operational_control: 'Operational control' boundary — the organisation includes in its inventory 100% of the emissions of sites/assets over which it has operational control (power to apply its own policies), regardless of ownership share. It is one of the three approaches allowed by the GHG Protocol (equity-share, financial control, operational control); ESRS E1 leaves the choice free provided the approach is explicitly declared.
esrs_e1: ESRS E1 — European Sustainability Reporting Standard on Climate Change (EFRAG 2023), applicable under CSRD. Covers: transition plan and policies (E1-1, E1-2), actions and targets (E1-3, E1-4), energy consumption (E1-5), Scope 1/2/3 GHG emissions with biogenic CO2 memo (E1-6), removals and mitigation projects including carbon credits (E1-7), internal carbon pricing (E1-8) and anticipated financial effects (E1-9). The generated PDF covers E1-5 and E1-6.
viano_banner_concept: Reduced operation — when a site has had a partial production stoppage, a raw YoY comparison is misleading. The banner flags the situation (MG-12, OI-2) to prompt the reader to normalise figures by operating hours or actual production.
biogenic_memo_concept: Memo disclosure — information accompanying but not summed into the totals. For biogenic CO2 this is mandatory under ESRS E1-6 and GHG Protocol §4.5 to avoid double-counting biological carbon already in the short cycle.
wtt_well_to_tank: WTT (Well-To-Tank) — upstream emissions of fuels: extraction, refining, transport to the consumption site. Reported under Scope 3 Category 3 and added to direct combustion emissions (Tank-To-Wheel/Stack) to obtain the total Well-To-Wheel/Stack.
uncertainty: Uncertainty — confidence interval associated with the emission factor (e.g. ±5% for ISPRA electricity, ±20% for spend-based LCA factors). It must be disclosed for the main factors (ESRS E1 AR 46 recommends qualitative or quantitative disclosure). For formal quantification the methodological reference is ISO 14064-1 Annex E (error propagation, Tier 1).
